title: Gasteranthus pansamalanus
intro: Gasteranthus pansamalanus, commonly known as the Pansamala Gasteranthus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Borgeaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gasteranthus pansamalanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
